title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A regional emergency-status protocol team. They need exact field handling, RFC 9112 body-length precedence, fixed and chunked content, trailers, response semantics, short-write-safe serialization, and round-trip evidence under changed fragmentation. Planned artifact: Deliver Bounded Framing, Chunking, and Response Engine with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Bound field bytes and count, reject obsolete folding and controls, redact secrets, and preserve only safe diagnostic evidence.
Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.
Keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Field Resource Disclosure
steps.instruction:
project case — A project protocol team handles case 9963 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Field Resource Disclosure.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bound field bytes and count, reject obsolete folding and controls, redact secrets, and preserve only safe diagnostic evidence. For Commit to the mission model · Project Field Resource Disclosure, keep Field Resource Disclosure connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Test an acceptance boundary · Project Serializer Roundtrip
steps.instruction:
project case — A project protocol team handles case 9971 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Round-trip supported messages through independent parse and serialize paths while preserving semantics, limits, and declared normalization.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
